---
title: "【AI Daily】AI World in Turmoil! Compute Wars Blaze to Space, Tech Titans' Rivalries Reshuffled, China's AI Autonomy Faces New Variables!"
date: "2026-04-28"
description: "The AI landscape is experiencing rapid upheaval"
tags: ["AI", "Daily Report", "Tech Trends"]
coverImage: "cover-2026-04-28-022928.png"
---

### 📋 Quick Highlights
- Meta is exploring space solar power to provide electricity for its high-energy-consuming AI data centers.
- China has officially blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, emphasizing technological sovereignty.
- Microsoft and OpenAI's exclusive partnership has ended, ushering the AI market into a multi-cloud battleground.
- Global investment in AI infrastructure is surging, with energy consumption now surpassing traditional oil and gas production investments.
- China's DeepSeek has released its new generation V4 model, emphasizing collaborative optimization with domestic hardware.
- Agentic AI applications are increasingly widespread in businesses in places like Hong Kong, transforming traditional work models.
- The AI market is expected to continue rapid growth, but companies need to be wary of the risks posed by "shadow AI."

### What Happened?

Hey everyone! Today (April 28, 2026) the AI world has been experiencing one bombshell after another, with developments more dramatic than a TV series! From tech giants looking to outer space for resources to feed AI's immense appetite for energy, to the undercurrents between China and the US in the AI sphere, and the major reshuffling of the partnership between Microsoft and OpenAI—it feels like the entire AI scene is embroiled in a massive showdown!

I've observed that AI is no longer a cool toy confined to laboratories; it's rapidly permeating every corner of our lives, from the apps on your phone to the data centers powering entire cities. It's omnipresent. However, this also brings forth many unprecedented challenges, such as enormous energy consumption, complex international political maneuvering, and the issue of "shadow AI" within corporate management.

### Simply Put, What Is This?

Imagine you have a super intelligent, but also super hungry, pet at home—AI. This pet consumes massive amounts of electricity every day to help you handle various complex tasks. Now, AI's "appetite" has grown so large that your household wiring isn't enough, and even the power supply for your entire neighborhood is starting to strain!

So, **Meta**, like a dedicated owner catering to this "power-hungry pet," is now looking to "outer space" for new energy sources, attempting to directly "harness" power from the sun and bring it back for use. Doesn't that sound like something out of a sci-fi movie?

Meanwhile, **Microsoft** and **OpenAI**, a partnership that was once inseparable, are like a celebrity duo whose "exclusive contract" has now become more flexible. OpenAI's AI models are no longer exclusively available through Microsoft Azure; they can also be found on AWS and Google Cloud. For businesses hoping to leverage AI, this is like having more supermarkets to buy your favorite products—more choices and fiercer market competition.

On the other side, **China** acts like a watchful guardian, directly stepping in to block Meta's acquisition of **Manus**, a Singaporean AI startup, to prevent its own "AI baby" from being taken away. This demonstrates that AI technology is now considered a national strategic asset; whoever controls it holds the future's discourse.

<Callout type="info" title="AI New Era Analogy">
Like how our smartphones have evolved from "communication tools" to "life managers," AI is transforming from a "lab monster" into a "world-changing tool." It must not only solve our problems but also address its own, such as energy crises and ethical challenges.
</Callout>

### According to Reports, Details Are as Follows

Today's most significant news points to several key trends in the AI industry:

#### 1. The AI Compute Arms Race and the Major Energy Challenge ⚡

Did you know? Global tech giants' spending on **AI infrastructure** now surpasses global investment in oil and gas production! This is no joke; according to the International Energy Agency (IEA), major tech companies' capital expenditures in **2025** exceeded **$400 billion**, and are projected to increase by another **75%** in **2026**.

Where is most of this money being invested? The answer is **data centers**. These "super-brains" built for AI model computation have an astronomical demand for electricity. The IEA predicts that global data center electricity consumption will double by **2030**, reaching **945 TWh**, accounting for nearly **3%** of global total electricity usage. Even more concerning, AI data centers in the UK are projected to emit **100 times** more carbon than previously estimated in the coming years, totaling **123 million tons**! This is almost equivalent to the carbon emissions of **2.7 million people**. In the US, data center energy demand is also expected to grow by **130%** by **2030**.

Facing this "power-hungry beast" trend, various giants are finding solutions. **Meta** announced a deal with **Overview Energy**, planning to obtain up to **1 GW** of electricity via **space solar power** by **2030**—equivalent to the output of a nuclear power plant! The idea is to place satellites in orbit to collect solar energy around the clock, convert it into infrared beams, and transmit it back to Earth, where existing ground solar power plants will receive and convert it into electricity. Concurrently, Meta is collaborating with **Noon Energy** to deploy **ultra-long-duration energy storage systems** of up to **1 GW/100 GWh**, ensuring 24/7 clean energy for AI data centers.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-04-20-020916"
  title="【AI Daily】AI World Explodes: From Robot Marathons to Data Center 'Nuclear Races,' Are Tech Giants Ready?"
  description="The global AI fervor continues as OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft announce their latest advancements. Behind the AI investment boom lie data center energy consumption and hardware supply chain challenges..."
  author="AI Daily Editorial Team"
  publisher="AI Daily"
/>

#### 2. The AI Market's Shifting Alliances? 🤝💔

Another bombshell in the market today is that **Microsoft** and **OpenAI**, two pivotal partners in the AI sphere, announced **adjustments to their agreement**. Simply put, Microsoft no longer holds an "exclusive license" for OpenAI's technology. This means OpenAI can now sell its product lines to customers on any cloud platform, including **Amazon Web Services (AWS)** and **Google Cloud**.

Microsoft will remain OpenAI's "primary cloud partner," and OpenAI's products will still be prioritized for launch on **Azure**. However, this new non-exclusive license will last until **2032**. Furthermore, Microsoft will no longer pay revenue share to OpenAI, while OpenAI will continue to pay revenue share to Microsoft until **2030**, albeit with an aggregate cap.

Behind this adjustment is OpenAI's strategic agreement with **Amazon** in **February** this year, a **$50 billion** deal making AWS the exclusive third-party cloud provider for OpenAI's enterprise-grade AI agent platform, **Frontier**. Now, AWS's **Amazon Bedrock** service has integrated OpenAI's open-weight models, allowing developers to use multiple leading AI models via a single API.

All this indicates that AI market competition has expanded from mere model capabilities to the integration of infrastructure and ecosystems.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-04-26-021149"
  title="【AI Daily】The AI World in Flux: Giants Splashing Cash to Stake Claims, Models Getting Smarter and Dumber, Are You Keeping Up?"
  description="The AI battlefield is truly exciting: OpenAI launches a more powerful GPT-5.5, Google DeepMind unleashes intelligent research agents."
  author="AI Daily Editorial Team"
  publisher="AI Daily"
/>

Meanwhile, **Google** hasn't been idle, investing a massive **$40 billion** in **Anthropic**, including a significant commitment to **TPU compute power** expected to be delivered starting **2027**. This AI-fueled compute arms race has truly drawn in all the major players.

<BookmarkCard
  href="/blog/2026-04-25-015754"
  title="【AI Daily】AI World Chaos: Google Pours $40 Billion into Anthropic, China's Chip Autonomy Flexes Muscles, Your AI Assistant Gets Smarter but Also 'Eats' More Memory!"
  description="The most anticipated news in the AI sector is Google's announcement of a massive **$40 billion** investment in Anthropic, bolstering its competitiveness in the enterprise AI market..."
  author="AI Daily Editorial Team"
  publisher="AI Daily"
/>

#### 3. China's AI Autonomous Innovation and "Firewall" Strategy 🇨🇳

Speaking of international competition, there's another significant piece of news today: China's top economic planning agency has officially **blocked Meta's plan to acquire Singaporean AI startup Manus**. Although Manus is currently headquartered in Singapore, its founders are from China, and the deal was reportedly worth over **$2 billion**. Beijing stated that this move aims to prevent "the outflow of talent, technological data, and capital" and to strengthen scrutiny over "strategically important areas" like AI. This is undoubtedly a strong signal from China in the core AI technology sector, indicating it will adopt a tougher stance to safeguard national interests.

Concurrently, Chinese domestic AI companies continue to demonstrate their capacity for autonomous innovation. Chinese startup **DeepSeek** today released preview versions of its flagship AI models: **V4 Flash** and **V4 Pro**. DeepSeek V4 not only excels in coding benchmarks and agent tasks but also boasts a **million-token context window**, meaning it can process longer texts and more complex conversations. More critically, DeepSeek V4 utilized **Huawei's Ascend 950 chips** during part of its training, which is highly significant for China in reducing its reliance on the US chip supply chain and promoting collaborative development of domestic hardware and software. The China Academy of Information and Communications Technology has also initiated DeepSeek-V4's domestic adaptation testing.

<InsightQuote
  type="insight"
  content="China blocking Meta's acquisition of Manus and the adaptation of the DeepSeek V4 model for domestic chips clearly indicate China's accelerating 'autonomous and controllable' strategy in AI. This is not just technological competition, but a reflection of national strategic security."
  author="Tech Storyteller"
  role="Senior Observer"
/>

#### 4. AI Applications Fully Blossom, From Experiment to Practicality 🚀

The practical implementation of AI is also accelerating. In Hong Kong, businesses are moving from experimental AI projects to large-scale deployment of **Agentic AI**. Companies like **AIA** and **AS Watson Group** have begun applying agentic AI in areas such as product training, lead management, automated claims processing, and customer self-service. This is like equipping every employee with a super-efficient AI assistant, allowing them to focus on more valuable work.

Moreover, AI applications are deepening across various industries. In China's Guangdong Province, known as the "World's Factory," the process of AI empowerment is accelerating. For instance, Shenzhen has pioneered the **nation's first large model for the judicial trial vertical** and activated an AI-assisted trial system, successfully shortening the average case closure time in Shenzhen courts by **38 days** in **2024**. This truly puts AI into practical use, solving real pain points in our lives.

<StatsHighlight
  title="AI Market Growth Forecast"
  stats={[
    { value: "USD 19.061 billion", label: "2026 Market Size" },
    { value: "USD 1.81 trillion", label: "2033 Projected Market Size" },
    { value: "38.1%", label: "2026-2033 CAGR" }
  ]}
/>

Meanwhile, **Lenovo's** research also indicates that while over **70%** of employees use AI weekly, a staggering **one-third** of this usage occurs without IT department oversight, creating what's known as "shadow AI," which introduces new governance and security risks for businesses.

### What Does This Mean for Us?

As ordinary people, what do these AI news stories mean for us?

Firstly, **your electricity bills might increase**. The enormous energy consumption of AI data centers will ultimately be passed on to power suppliers, and then to us, the consumers. However, Meta's exploration of nascent energy solutions like space solar power might, in the long run, bring us cleaner, more stable electricity, although that still sounds a bit distant.

Secondly, **your way of working will be completely transformed by AI**. The rise of agentic AI is like equipping you with a "super-powered assistant." In the future, many repetitive, time-consuming tasks can be handed over to AI assistants. This doesn't mean AI will steal your job, but rather that your job content will shift, focusing more on creativity, strategy, and human interaction.

<StepGuide
  title="How to Embrace Agentic AI?"
  steps={[
    { title: "Learn to Collaborate with AI", description: "View AI as your intelligent assistant, learning how to effectively give commands and utilize its output." },
    { title: "Focus on Improving Soft Skills", description: "AI excels at hard skills; we need to strengthen soft skills like communication, problem-solving, and creativity." },
    { title: "Maintain a Habit of Lifelong Learning", description: "AI technology evolves rapidly; continuous learning of new tools and concepts is key to staying competitive." }
  ]}
/>

Thirdly, **the landscape of the tech industry will continue to reshuffle, bringing more innovation**. The adjustment of the Microsoft-OpenAI agreement means OpenAI's technology will be accessible to more platforms and developers, stimulating more innovative applications. At the same time, major giants, vying for leadership in AI infrastructure, will continually invest huge sums in R&D, ultimately benefiting the broad user base.

Finally, **inter-state tech competition will intensify, with far-reaching impacts**. China blocking Meta's acquisition of Manus and DeepSeek V4's adaptation for domestic chips clearly demonstrate the importance various countries place on AI technological dominance. This will not only affect the tech products we can use but may even influence international trade and cooperation.

### Insider's Deep Analysis

From the perspective of a senior investigative journalist, I see that this is actually an inevitable progression of AI development. When a technology begins to move towards large-scale application, it inevitably touches upon several core issues: **resources, governance, and geopolitics**.

The **resource issue**, particularly energy, has become the "limiting factor" for AI development. If we cannot find sufficient and sustainable energy to feed these AI models, the pace of AI development will be severely restricted. Meta's space solar power project, though it sounds somewhat crazy, is a true reflection of tech giants seeking "Plan B" under extreme pressure. This also highlights the importance of next-generation energy technologies, such as nuclear fusion and novel energy storage, for the AI era.

The **governance issue**, especially the existence of "shadow AI," is a ticking time bomb for businesses. When employees extensively use AI tools without IT department oversight, potential data leaks, compliance risks, and uncontrolled costs can spread like wildfire. Companies must establish more robust AI governance frameworks; this is no longer an option but a survival imperative.

The impact of **geopolitics** is even more evident. China's intervention in the Meta acquisition and DeepSeek's emphasis on domestic chips both prove that AI is no longer purely commercial competition but has escalated to a national strategic level. In the future, cooperation and confrontation among nations in the AI field will become more frequent, profoundly reshaping the global technology supply chain.

<InsightQuote
  type="insight"
  content="AI's development has transitioned from the 'technological breakthrough' phase to the 'ecosystem and governance' phase. Those who can effectively integrate resources, build healthy partnerships, and address the accompanying ethical and security challenges will be the ones to triumph in the future AI race."
  author="Tech Storyteller"
  role="Senior Analyst"
/>

### One-Liner Summary

Today's AI world is like a teenager—full of growing pains and infinite possibilities; it must address its own "energy anxiety," navigate the complex rivalries and alliances within its "friend group," and learn how to secure its best interests on the "international stage." For us, this is an era brimming with challenges, but also immense opportunities. Are you ready for the monumental changes AI will bring?

---
